content.text:
<user_query>

dd this to your  ~/.hammerspoon/init.lua 

hs.hotkey.bind({"cmd","shift"}, "P", function()
  hs.application.launchOrFocus("Perplexity")
end)

</user_query>

Let me check the current state of the file first.

The user wants me to add a Hammerspoon hotkey binding to their `~/.hammerspoon/init.lua` file. Let me first check if the file exists and read its contents.

The binding is already in the file. It looks like it was previously added. The `~/.hammerspoon/init.lua` already contains the exact snippet:

```3:5:/Users/HanHu/.hammerspoon/init.lua
hs.hotkey.bind({"cmd","shift"}, "P", function()
    hs.application.launchOrFocus("Perplexity")
  end)
```

No changes needed — it's already there. You can reload Hammerspoon (Cmd+Shift+R from the Hammerspoon menu or run `hs.reload()` in the console) to make sure it's active.

<user_query>
Keep the script running in the background. Currently, it dies after re-logging.
</user_query>

The user wants Hammerspoon to keep running in the background and survive re-logging (logging out and back in). This means they need Hammerspoon to auto-start on login. There are a few ways to do this:

1. Hammerspoon has a built-in setting to "Launch Hammerspoon at login" in its preferences
2. You can also add it as a Login Item via macOS System Settings
3. Or you can use `launchctl` with a LaunchAgent plist

The most reliable approach is to either:
- Enable the auto-launch setting within Hammerspoon itself via the Lua API: `hs.autoLaunch(true)`


- Or create a LaunchAgent plist

I should add `hs.autoLaunch(true)` to their init.lua as the simplest solution, and I can also mention `hs.menuIcon(false)` to hide the menu bar icon if they prefer. Let me check if there's already a LaunchAgent configured for Hammerspoon.
The simplest fix is to add `hs.autoLaunch(true)` to your `init.lua`. This tells Hammerspoon to register itself as a login item so it starts automatically after logging in.
Now let me reload Hammerspoon so the change takes effect.
Done. I added `hs.autoLaunch(true)` to the top of your `init.lua` and reloaded the config. Hammerspoon will now automatically launch when you log in, so it will survive re-logging.
